title: approvals — tenancy hooks
description: Per-tenant lifecycle callbacks (`#[AsTenancyHook]`) this module contributes. Two hooks: warm the tenant's ApproverSelectorEngine delegation-substitution cache on tenant activation, purge the same cache on tenant deactivation. Distinct from listeners.json — hooks run on the tenancy-lifecycle dispatcher (not the event dispatcher), so they run BEFORE any downstream module's tenant-activation logic can query the engine.

hooks.name: WarmDelegationSubstitutionCache
hooks.purpose: On tenant activation (each request context), pre-load the tenant's active RoleDelegation index into the request-scoped ApproverSelectorEngine cache. Halves the latency of the first instance-create in the request. Read-only; no writes. Cost: 1 SELECT (a few ms). Guarded by `approvals.delegation_substitution_enabled` feature flag.

hooks.name: PurgeApprovalCaches
hooks.purpose: On tenant deactivation (usually per-request cleanup or manual tenant-switch in support tooling), purges the request-scoped caches — delegation-substitution index, compiled expression AST cache-slice, tenant-scoped ApprovalPresenter memoisation. Prevents cross-tenant contamination in support-agent impersonation flows.

notes: Both hooks are IDEMPOTENT — activating a tenant twice or deactivating an already-deactivated tenant is a no-op.
The delegation-substitution cache is REQUEST-SCOPED (per-Octane-request) — not persisted between requests. Warming on activation trades a small startup cost for consistent hot-path latency.
The Approvable Actions Registry is APPLICATION-WIDE (not per-tenant) — it's populated at BOOT from #[AsApprovableAction] discovery. No per-tenant warming needed. Every tenant sees the same registry.
